summary: The live status summary is largely guide-aligned: StatusSummary is a pure derivation PORO correctly placed in app/lib with a query-style .for; tone is sourced from the single StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES table so dot and badge can't diverge; broadcasts target the smallest DOM unit (dom_id(pipeline,:summary) / dom_id(phase,:column)); both surfaces render true state on load with streams as enhancement; the summary dot is aria-hidden with the sentence carrying meaning inside an aria-live=polite region; Tailwind type scale, eyebrow label, Card, and semantic status colors all match the UI guide; the aborted->gray reclassification is a coherent same-PR guide update. Two backend-guide deviations block a pass: (1) Phases::Advance performs multiple writes without owning a transaction, and (2) BroadcastColumn (and Advance's broadcasts) are invoked inside ManagerTick's open transaction, contradicting the guide's after-commit rule. One minor UI-component drift on the gate approve button/input.

findings.issue: Phases::Advance performs two writes on the advance path (next_phase.update!(status: "running") then pipeline.update!(current_phase:, status:)) with no ApplicationRecord.transaction wrapper. backend-guide.md ('Services') states: 'Services own transactions (ApplicationRecord.transaction) — a business action is atomic or it isn't done.' Because Phases::Approve calls Advance AFTER its own transaction commits, a failure of the second update from the human-approval path leaves the next phase running while the pipeline still points at the prior phase — a partially-advanced, inconsistent board state the guide's atomicity rule exists to prevent. Wrap the advance writes in a transaction (from the ManagerTick path they are already inside the outer tick transaction, but Advance must guarantee its own atomicity for its other caller).

BroadcastColumn.call is invoked inside ManagerTick's open ApplicationRecord.transaction (escalate at ~line 122; apply_gate human branch at ~line 179; and Advance's broadcasts via settle_convergence -> reach_consensus -> advance_pipeline -> Advance.call, all still inside the transaction block at lines 41-49). backend-guide.md ('Services' / 'Real-time (Turbo) conventions') is explicit: 'Broadcasts/jobs happen only after the write commits (after_commit semantics — use ActiveRecord.after_all_transactions_commit or enqueue inside the service after the transaction block).' broadcast_replace_later_to enqueues a Solid Queue job that commits to the separate queue DB independently of the main transaction, so it can run before (or despite a rollback of) the tick transaction and repaint stale phase state — the very 'invisible until reload' failure the feature is meant to fix. Note the tick's own broadcast_affected and Pipelines::BroadcastStatus are already correctly placed AFTER the transaction block, so the in-transaction BroadcastColumn calls are an internal inconsistency; move them (and Advance's broadcasts) to after commit.

The gate approve button and note input are styled ad hoc rather than from the defined shared components. ui-style-guide.md ('Core components' / 'Buttons') defines the primary button as 'bg-indigo-600 text-white hover:bg-indigo-700 rounded-md px-3 py-2 text-sm font-semibold', but the gate button uses px-2 py-1.5 text-xs; the guide's form input spec is 'rounded-md ring-1 ring-gray-300 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-600', but the note field uses ring-amber-600/30. The guide's 'one source of truth per component' principle wants these built as the shared button/input (or an explicitly-approved compact variant) rather than inline overrides, even in the dense banner context.
